description_long: This sculpture is a high-relief marble carving set into a rectangular stone block, depicting a partially reclined human figure draped in classical robes. The figure's head is bowed and partially obscured, with one arm bent and the other extended, suggesting a pose of rest or contemplation. The carving is deeply recessed into the block, creating dramatic shadows and a sense of depth. The surface of the marble is weathered, with visible chisel marks and some discoloration, indicating age and exposure. The back and sides of the block are rough and unfinished, contrasting with the more detailed front. The overall appearance is reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman funerary or commemorative reliefs.
description: A weathered marble relief sculpture of a draped, reclining human figure carved into a rectangular stone block. The front is detailed, while the sides and back are rough and unfinished.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed relief of the draped human figure, with the head bowed and arms positioned in a relaxed pose within the recessed block.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the depth of the relief and the rough, unfinished stone extending behind the figure.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the block, which is mostly flat and uncarved, with the figure's legs and feet partially visible in relief.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the thickness of the block and the protruding form of the figure's arm and drapery.
attribute_subject_matter: reclining draped human figure in classical style
attribute_location_context: No clear environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
